headline: Agent memory and production evaluation dominate the window as builders converge on persistent state and continuous testing.
body: The day clusters around agent persistence: multiple talks treat memory and durable state as the core gap, with note-to-memory pipelines for agents, an argument that coding agents are experts with amnesia, and a case for anchoring agent identity in append-only session logs rather than orchestration layers. On the evaluation side, multi-step workflows, human-in-the-loop, and observability all break out together as Meta details continuous online evaluation pipelines for detecting drift and hallucinations beyond offline benchmarks. New open agentic coding models arrived in the Ornith family (9B to 397B, MIT-licensed) for local deployment, and Google Cloud extended data agents with Model Context Protocol integrations. A separate report points to Anthropic's pulled Fable 5 model possibly returning amid national security scrutiny.

cited_items.title: Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
Ornith (35B,9B) + Hermes,Zed: THE FULLY PRIVATE LOCAL AGENT is ACTUALLY HERE!
Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
